message.content:
{
  "homework": {
    "sessionInformation": {
      "sessionNumber": "1",
      "sessionTitle": "A Truly Beautiful Mind",
      "subject": "English Language and Literature",
      "grade": "Class IX / 9",
      "difficultyLevel": "Balanced",
      "learningPace": "Supportive",
      "estimatedHomeworkDuration": "45 minutes"
    },
    "homework": [
      {
        "id": 1,
        "type": "Reading Comprehension & Vocabulary",
        "title": "Understanding the Text and Key Terms",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["Identify key vocabulary related to Einstein's life", "Understand the central theme of the text"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Einstein's childhood", "Key vocabulary words", "The concept of a 'beautiful mind'"],
        "difficulty": "Medium",
        "marks": 10,
        "estimatedTime": "15 minutes",
        "instructions": "Read the text 'A Truly Beautiful Mind' carefully. Answer the following questions based on your understanding.",
        "question": "Part A: Find five words from the text that describe Einstein's personality or his work (e.g., genius, eccentric). Write their meanings in your own words.\n\nPart B: Why does the author call Einstein a 'truly beautiful mind'? Support your answer with two examples from the text.",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Write your answers in complete sentences.",
        "visualRequirement": "None",
        "expectedResponse": "Part A: Students should list words like 'genius', 'eccentric', 'kind', 'humble' with correct definitions. Part B: Answers should reflect that Einstein was a great scientist who used his knowledge for peace and humanitarian causes, not just for fame or power."
      },
      {
        "id": 2,
        "type": "Grammar & Sentence Construction",
        "title": "Using Past Tense and Descriptive Adjectives",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["Apply past tense verbs correctly", "Use descriptive adjectives to characterize a person"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Past tense usage", "Adjective usage in context"],
        "difficulty": "Medium",
        "marks": 10,
        "estimatedTime": "15 minutes",
        "instructions": "Rewrite the following sentences using the correct past tense forms of the verbs given in brackets. Then, add one descriptive adjective to each sentence.",
        "question": "1. Einstein (live) in a small house in Berlin.\n2. He (publish) his theory of relativity in 1905.\n3. The students (listen) to his lecture with great interest.\n4. His wife (support) him during difficult times.",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Write the corrected sentences below.",
        "visualRequirement": "None",
        "expectedResponse": "1. Einstein lived in a small house in Berlin. (e.g., quiet, humble)\n2. He published his theory of relativity in 1905. (e.g., famous, groundbreaking)\n3. The students listened to his lecture with great interest. (e.g., attentive, eager)\n4. His wife supported him during difficult times. (e.g., devoted, loving)"
      },
      {
        "id": 3,
        "type": "Creative Writing & Reflection",
        "title": "Letter to Einstein: A Message of Gratitude",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["Express personal reflection on the text", "Apply creative writing skills"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Einstein's contributions", "Personal values and gratitude"],
        "difficulty": "High",
        "marks": 15,
        "estimatedTime": "20 minutes",
        "instructions": "Imagine you are a student who has just read about Einstein's life and his contribution to science and peace. Write a letter to him expressing your admiration for his 'beautiful mind'. Include how his life inspires you.",
        "question": "Write a formal or semi-formal letter to Albert Einstein (approx. 150 words).",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Use the standard format of a letter (Sender's Address, Date, Salutation, Body, Complimentary Close, Signature).",
        "visualRequirement": "None",
        "expectedResponse": "A well-structured letter with a clear salutation and closing. The body should mention specific aspects of Einstein's life (e.g., his humility, his scientific work, his peace efforts) and explain how these qualities inspire the writer."
      },
      {
        "id": 4,
        "type": "Critical Thinking & Real-World Connection",
        "title": "Connecting Science to Humanity",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["Analyze the relationship between science and ethics", "Connect text themes to real-world scenarios"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Science for peace", "Ethical responsibility of scientists"],
        "difficulty": "High",
        "marks": 10,
        "estimatedTime": "15 minutes",
        "instructions": "The text mentions that Einstein used his fame to help others. Discuss the following question in a short paragraph.",
        "question": "Do you think a scientist's work should be judged only by their discoveries, or also by how they use their knowledge for the good of humanity? Give one example from history or current events to support your view.",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Write a paragraph of 80-100 words.",
        "visualRequirement": "None",
        "expectedResponse": "A reasoned argument stating that scientific contributions should be evaluated alongside their ethical impact. The student should provide a relevant example (e.g., medical research for public health vs. weapons development)."
      },
      {
        "id": 5,
        "type": "Parent Engagement Activity",
        "title": "Family Discussion: What Makes a 'Beautiful Mind'?",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["Connect learning to family values", "Oral communication practice"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Values of kindness and humility", "Role models in society"],
        "difficulty": "Low",
        "marks": 5,
        "estimatedTime": "10 minutes",
        "instructions": "Discuss with your parents or guardians: 'Who is a person you admire for their character rather than just their achievements?' Share one story about them.",
        "question": "Record the name of the person and one sentence about why they are admired.",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Write the name and the reason below.",
        "visualRequirement": "None",
        "expectedResponse": "A student response identifying a family member, teacher, or public figure known for kindness, integrity, or service, with a brief explanation."
      }
    ],
    "summary": {
      "totalQuestions": 5,
      "totalMarks": 50,
      "estimatedCompletionTime": "75 minutes",
      "learningOutcomesCovered": [
        "Identify key vocabulary related to Einstein's life",
        "Understand the central theme of the text",
        "Apply past tense verbs correctly",
        "Use descriptive adjectives to characterize a person",
        "Express personal reflection on the text",
        "Apply creative writing skills",
        "Analyze the relationship between science and ethics",
        "Connect text themes to real-world scenarios",
        "Connect learning to family values",
        "Oral communication practice"
      ],
      "topicsCovered": [
        "Einstein's childhood",
        "Key vocabulary words",
        "The concept of a 'beautiful mind'",
        "Past tense usage",
        "Adjective usage in context",
        "Einstein's contributions",
        "Personal values and gratitude",
        "Science for peace",
        "Ethical responsibility of scientists",
        "Values of kindness and humility"
      ],
      "subtopicsCovered": [
        "Vocabulary definitions",
        "Textual evidence analysis",
        "Grammar application in sentences",
        "Letter writing format",
        "Critical argumentation",
        "Family discussion on values"
      ],
      "taskDistribution": {
        "Reading Comprehension & Vocabulary": 1,
        "Grammar & Sentence Construction": 1,
        "Creative Writing & Reflection": 1,
        "Critical Thinking & Real-World Connection": 1,
        "Parent Engagement Activity": 1
      },
      "homeExperimentIncluded": false,
      "parentEngagementIncluded": true
    }
  }
}
